purpose: Establish a shared WhatsApp group where two or more AI agents (AGYs) can monitor, read, and send messages to communicate with each other and their users.

technical_stack.authentication: QR Code scan -> Persisted in local userDataDir (whatsapp_auth)

development_process: 1. Explored the WhatsApp Business Cloud API but rejected it due to Meta's strict verification requirements and costs.
2. Pivoted to whatsapp-web.js, reverse-engineering the WhatsApp Web protocol.
3. Overcame ESM/CommonJS import conflicts by importing default `pkg` and extracting modules (e.g., MessageMedia).
4. Resolved group creation by translating complex @lid (hidden identifiers) into standard @c.us phone numbers using getChats() resolution.
5. Resolved a crash where the API failed to fetch contact info for self-sent messages (outgoing from a linked device) by wrapping msg.getContact() in a try/catch.
6. Implemented a highly token-efficient 'exit-on-message' background listener loop for real-time monitoring.

how_it_works: The script connects to WhatsApp and attaches a listener to the `message_create` event.
The `message_create` event is specifically used instead of `message` to ensure we catch self-sent outgoing messages as well as incoming ones.
When a message arrives in the target group, the script prints a structured JSON payload to stdout and IMMEDIATELY calls process.exit(0).
An AI Subagent runs this script as a background task. Because the script exits immediately on message receipt, the system instantly wakes the Subagent up without any buffering delay.
The Subagent processes the message, alerts the main agent/user, and then instantly restarts the script to listen for the next one.

instructions_for_sals_agy.step_2: Authenticate via QR code using Sal's phone. (Sal is already an admin in the group).
instructions_for_sals_agy.step_3: Implement a script to send messages: `client.sendMessage('120363427507526294@g.us', 'Hello from Sal\'s AGY!')`.
instructions_for_sals_agy.step_4: Implement the 'exit-on-message' listener loop on the `message_create` event to react to messages in real-time.
instructions_for_sals_agy.step_5: Once running, both AGYs will be in the chat reading and writing synchronously!
